name: Magento (Adobe Commerce)
description: Adobe Commerce (formerly Magento Commerce) is a headless commerce platform for delivering personalized commerce experiences across touchpoints, with tools to create and manage storefronts, commerce content, merchandising, and core operations like product catalogs, orders, and customers.

auth_methods.note: Official REST authentication docs list Integration tokens (used as a Bearer token in the Authorization header), OAuth 1.0a for third-party apps, and Adobe IMS (OAuth 2.0) for Adobe Commerce as a Cloud Service. (Tool schema supports oauth2/token; oauth1 not represented.)

credential_access.note: The developer guide shows how to create integrations and obtain integration/admin tokens from the Magento Admin (System > Extensions > Integrations), which a developer can do on a self-hosted Magento/Open Source instance without human approval. (Adobe Commerce Cloud as-a-service variants require an Adobe license/Cloud account; see product page.)

api_surface.note: The Commerce Web API docs describe REST and GraphQL APIs (and historically SOAP) covering core objects (products, customers, orders, inventory), indicating broad coverage of product objects.

buildability.note: Developers can self-host Magento Open Source or run a local/dev instance and create integrations/admin tokens to access REST/GraphQL APIs per docs, so building integrations/agent toolkits is possible today. (If targeting Adobe Commerce Cloud, a licensed Cloud account is required.)
